document.text:
DISCHARGE SUMMARY

Patient: Monica Johnson        MRN: 0947918871
DOB: 05/29/2024                       Age: 23
Admitted: 03/25/2019                  Discharged: 06/26/2018
Attending: Dr. Debra Lloyd, MD
Service: Cardiology, Allen and Sons

HPI: Mr. Hollingsworth is a 23 male with a history of CAD s/p
PCI 2019, HTN, and HLD who presented to the ED on Jun 11, 2024 with substernal
chest pain radiating to his left jaw. Wife (Angel, +1-812-344-5976x897) accompanied
him. Troponin peaked at 1.84.

Hospital course: Underwent cardiac cath on hospital day 1 with drug-eluting
stent to the mid-LAD. No further events. Tolerated aspirin/clopidogrel
without bleeding. Echo on 06/12/2020 showed EF 45-50%.

Discharge meds: ASA 81mg daily, clopidogrel 75mg daily, atorvastatin 80mg
qHS, metoprolol succinate 50mg daily, lisinopril 10mg daily.

Follow-up: Cardiology with Dr. Subramanian on 01/18/2026 at May 15, 2022,
clinic at 93139 Chavez Oval, Durham NC 27710. PCP Dr. James Morris at Wilson LLC within 1 week. Patient may be reached at +1-399-309-1211x9075 or
ataylor@example.net.

HISTORY & PHYSICAL

Patient: Monica Johnson           Date: 07/16/2018 0815
DOB: 05/29/2024                           MRN: 95733275186
Admitting MD: Dr. Jason Banks
Location: Lin, Allen and Lopez, 91244 Greene Walks, 8976 Robert Flat Apt. 093

CC: 23 G2P1 at 38+4 weeks presenting in active labor.

HPI: Patient began having regular contractions at 0430 this morning, q4
minutes. SROM at 0610. Husband Jessica drove her in from their home in
94773 Richards Crest. Prenatal care with Dr. Morgan Reid at Williams, Brown and Hernandez, last visit
04/22/2024 unremarkable.

PMH: Mild gestational diabetes (diet-controlled), seasonal allergies.
PSH: Cesarean Jul 19, 2017 for breech (son Mary, now 23).
Meds: PNV daily, metformin held since labor onset.
Allergies: Sulfa (rash).

Social: Lives with husband at 751 Mary Dam, 0148 Olson Causeway Suite 212.
Works as elementary school teacher (Rodriguez, King and Orozco). Cell:
+1-753-647-6579x861. Emergency contact: mother, Theresa Romero, (617)380-5097x06859
(currently in 9402 Lee Plaza).

Exam: VS 124/78, HR 92, T 98.6. Cervix 5 cm / 80% / 0 station.

Plan: Admit L&D bed 7, anesthesia consult for epidural, GBS+ — start
penicillin per protocol, continuous fetal monitoring.

EMERGENCY DEPARTMENT NOTE

Patient: Monica Johnson           Acct: 16025470900247
DOB: 05/29/2024                      MRN: 4859354
Arrival: 07/09/2023 Sep 16, 2021            Mode: Private vehicle
Provider: Dr. John Paul, ED attending

CC: Right-sided abdominal pain x 6 hours.

HPI: 46M presents with sudden-onset RLQ pain that began around 1700 today
while at work (Pemberton Auto Body, 13209 Gavin Inlet, 9768 Shepard Parkway Apt. 633). Pain rated
8/10, sharp, associated with one episode of nonbilious emesis. No fevers
documented at home, but felt warm. Last meal lunch at noon — Bojangles.
Drove himself to Everett, Greene and Williams when pain worsened.

PMH: Obesity (BMI 34), prediabetes. No prior surgeries.
Meds: None.
Social: Lives alone, divorced. One adult daughter (Jeremy, contacted at
001-213-394-7735x9315 per pt request). Smokes 1/2 ppd, drinks weekends.

Exam: T 100.4, HR 108, BP 142/88. Tender RLQ with rebound, +Jones Inc: WBC 16.2, lactate 1.4, lipase WNL.
Imaging: CT abdomen/pelvis with contrast read by Dr. Claire Farmer at Jan 23, 2023 —
acute appendicitis with periappendiceal stranding, no perf.

Disposition: Surgery consult to Dr. Scott Rhodes, OR booked Oct 25, 2025. NPO,
IV ceftriaxone + metronidazole, 1L LR. Daughter notified.

document.text:
OPERATIVE NOTE

Patient: Monica Johnson       MRN: 3702808
DOB: 06/21/2017                        Date of surgery: 11/20/2023
Surgeon: Dr. Jennifer Medina                 Assistant: Dr. John Paul, PGY-3
Anesthesia: Dr. Clifford Rodriguez (general endotracheal)
Facility: Johnson PLC, OR 4

Pre-op diagnosis: Symptomatic cholelithiasis.
Post-op diagnosis: Same.
Procedure: Laparoscopic cholecystectomy with intraoperative cholangiogram.

Indications: 23 with 4-month history of post-prandial RUQ pain. Ultrasound
on 06/18/2017 showed multiple gallstones, no ductal dilation. Discussed
with patient and her son Colleen (POA, contact 559-576-8976x816) at clinic
visit 05/15/2022.

Findings: Inflamed gallbladder with multiple stones; cholangiogram normal.
Specimens: Gallbladder to pathology (specimen ID 659990313130).
EBL: <10 mL.  Fluids: 1100 mL LR.  UOP: 250 mL.
Complications: None.

Disposition: PACU then short-stay unit, expected DC tomorrow morning.
Husband Jamie to drive home to 139 Chavez Oval, 593 Morgan Roads.
Follow-up in clinic with Dr. Ashley in 2 weeks. Office: 7913211907.

document.text:
DAILY PROGRESS NOTE — HOSPITAL DAY 4

Patient: Monica Johnson                  MRN: 236979717
DOB: 06/21/2017                            Room: 7N-412
Attending: Dr. Karla Higgins              Service: General medicine
Date: Sep 16, 2021

Subjective: Pt reports feeling "much better" today. No chest pain, no
SOB at rest. Slept 5 hours, woke for 2g vanco at 0400. Wife John visited
yesterday evening; she will pick him up on discharge to their home at
13209 Gavin Inlet, 9768 Shepard Parkway Apt. 633.

Objective: Afebrile x48h. T 98.2, BP 118/72, HR 78, SpO2 96% Everett, Greene and Williams (08/13/2018): WBC 9.1 (down from 18.4 on admission), Cr 1.0, K 4.1.
Blood cultures from 11/06/2025 NGTD x72h.
Micro: MSSA from initial cx, sensitive to nafcillin — switched 08/21/2023.

Assessment/Plan:
1) MSSA bacteremia, source likely cellulitis right lower extremity —
   continuing nafcillin 2g IV q4h, day 5 of planned 14-day course.
   ID consult Dr. Carl Watkins agrees with current regimen.
2) HTN — controlled on amlodipine.
3) Disposition — case management (Shawn Washington, ext 4421) arranging
   home infusion with PICC. Anticipate DC tomorrow if stable. Daughter
   Elizabeth (cell 001-431-359-9091) will be primary contact for home health.

Pt and family agreeable. Spent 35 minutes at bedside.

— Lauren Martinez, MD

NURSING SHIFT NOTE — 1900-0700

Pt Monica Johnson (MRN 29622464), 23 admitted 06/21/2017 for
DKA, now on insulin gtt at 4 units/hr titrating per protocol. AOx3 at
shift start, cooperative.

2030 — BG 312, K 3.9. Continued gtt, started D5½NS at 125/hr per Dr.
Kavanaugh's order. Mother Karla (call from 001-438-631-3132x091) called twice
asking about transfer to step-down; updated her, will call back when bed
assigned.

2245 — Pt c/o nausea, given Zofran 4mg IV. Boyfriend Sheena arrived and
stayed until Nov 28, 2019. Pt's address per face sheet: 8976 Robert Flat Apt. 093,
94773 Richards Crest.

0145 — BG 188, transitioned to subcutaneous regimen per endocrine
(Dr. Morgan Reid). D/c'd insulin gtt at 0220 after 2-hour overlap.
Pt slept from 0245-0500.

0530 — Voided 400mL clear yellow. AM labs drawn, sent to lab. BG 142.
Pt asked about her work — Williams, Brown and Hernandez, manager Lauren Martinez,
work cell 001-807-951-1727x60148. She wants discharge paperwork stating she was
admitted so she can excuse her shifts.

Plan for day shift: Endo to round, expect transfer to 5-South.

Brian Adams, RN BSN — Reg# 176368410503

document.text:
DIAGNOSTIC IMAGING — FINAL REPORT

Patient name: Monica Johnson       Patient ID: 16412526979848215
DOB: 05/29/2024                           Sex: M
Exam: MRI Brain w/ and w/o contrast       Accession: A26104772
Performed: 03/25/2019 Jun 26, 2018 at Sanchez, Paul and Davis, 20912 Mays Knolls, 8976 Robert Flat Apt. 093
Ordering provider: Dr. Jessica Ball (Neurology, oncall pager 539-347-7359)

CLINICAL HISTORY: 23 with 3-week history of progressive left-hand weakness
and aphasia. Outpatient evaluation referred by PCP Dr. Morgan Reid on
06/22/2022.

COMPARISON: CT head from 05/02/2022 at Jones Inc (limited
images obtained from outside courier).

TECHNIQUE: Multiplanar, multisequence MRI of the brain pre- and
post-administration of 10 mL Gadavist IV.

FINDINGS: 3.4 x 2.8 x 3.1 cm heterogeneously enhancing mass centered in
the right frontal lobe, crossing the genu of the corpus callosum.
Surrounding vasogenic edema with mass effect on the right lateral
ventricle, 4 mm midline shift to the left. Restricted diffusion within
the enhancing rim.

IMPRESSION:
1. Right frontal mass with imaging characteristics most consistent with
   high-grade glioma. Differential includes metastasis or lymphoma.
2. Recommend neurosurgical and neuro-oncology consultation. Findings
   discussed with Dr. Claire by phone at Dec 15, 2023 on 03/25/2019.

Electronically signed: Emily Gonzales — Neuroradiology
Report finalized: 03/25/2019 Feb 06, 2023

document.text:
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Monica Johnson           Specimen: SP-26-018824
DOB: 07/16/2018                            Date received: 05/29/2024 Mar 25, 2019
Sex: F                                     MRN: 73938836091
Submitting MD: Dr. Debra Lloyd (general surgery)
Facility: Allen and Sons, 91244 Greene Walks, 8976 Robert Flat Apt. 093

Clinical history: 23 with 2.1 cm left breast mass detected on screening
mammogram 08/29/2023; biopsy positive for invasive ductal carcinoma. Now
s/p left lumpectomy with sentinel node biopsy on 05/29/2024.

Specimens received:
A. Left breast lumpectomy (oriented with sutures per Dr. Jennifer)
B. Sentinel lymph node #1
C. Sentinel lymph node #2

Gross description: A. 4.2 x 3.8 x 2.6 cm fibrofatty tissue with single
suture marking superior margin. On sectioning, ill-defined firm tan-white
mass measuring 1.9 cm. B. 1.4 x 1.0 x 0.7 cm tan-pink lymph node, bisected.
C. 0.9 x 0.7 x 0.5 cm tan-pink lymph node.

Microscopic / final diagnosis:
A. Left breast, lumpectomy: Invasive ductal carcinoma, Nottingham grade 2,
   1.9 cm. Margins negative; closest 4 mm (deep). LVI not identified.
   ER 95% positive, PR 80% positive, HER2 1+ (negative).
B. Sentinel node #1: 1 of 1 negative for metastasis (0/1).
C. Sentinel node #2: 1 of 1 negative for metastasis (0/1).

Pathologic stage: pT1c pN0.

Patient/oncology contact: Tumor board scheduled 05/06/2024 1700, Conf Rm
B-2. Med onc Dr. Jason Hill to follow. Patient cell 335.393.1599x0912,
preferred email chambersscott@example.net.

Pathologist: Dr. Chad Li, MD, PhD — Electronic signature on file
Reported: 04/27/2017 Feb 24, 2021

document.text:
CARDIOLOGY CONSULT NOTE

Patient: Monica Johnson         MRN: 0947918871
DOB: 05/29/2024                          Age: 23
Date: 03/25/2019
Consulting MD: Dr. Jason Banks, Cardiology
Requested by: Dr. Lauren Lin (Hospitalist)

Reason for consult: New atrial fibrillation with RVR.

HPI: Mr. John is an 23 retired postal worker admitted
yesterday from Shepherd Inc (44597 Robbins Underpass) with pneumonia. Today developed new-onset AF with rates
130-150s. Granddaughter and POA Melissa Vargas (cell (581)663-3993x0931) was
notified by the floor nurse.

Past cardiac hx: HTN x 30 years, mild LV hypertrophy on echo 2024.
Never had documented arrhythmia previously.

Meds on admission: Lisinopril 20mg, atorvastatin 40mg, ASA 81mg,
multivitamin. Per his daughter Shawn (called from 59315 Carol Drive Apt. 190,
+1-975-211-7276x01486), patient also takes herbal teas.

Exam: 23M alert, oriented to person and place. Irregularly irregular pulse
~140. Lungs with right basilar crackles. No edema.

ECG: AF, rate 138, no acute ischemic changes.
Echo (today, bedside): EF preserved, LA dilated 4.8 cm.

Assessment/Plan:
1) New AF likely precipitated by acute infection. Rate control with IV
   diltiazem 10mg now, transition to PO 30mg q6h. Goal HR <110 at rest.
2) CHA2DS2-VASc = 4. Recommend apixaban 5mg BID once renal function
   confirmed stable. Discussed risk/benefit with patient and Brandon by
   phone at Nov 12, 2017.
3) Treat underlying pneumonia per primary team.
4) Outpatient cardiology follow-up with me — Acevedo Inc, 3953 Yates Mill Suite 579. Front desk 001-286-715-9438x617, ext 2 for scheduling.

Thank you for the kind consultation.

— A. Standing-Bear, MD, FACC

document.text:
TELEHEALTH VISIT NOTE

Patient: Monica Johnson         DOB: 07/16/2018
Visit type: Video (Zoom for Healthcare)   Date/time: 05/29/2024 Mar 25, 2019 PT
MRN: 75695428016763                   Insurance: Sanchez, Paul and Davis, ID 206641669056
Provider: Dr. Lisa Johnson, family medicine
Patient location at time of visit: Home — 44597 Robbins Underpass

CC: Medication refill, anxiety follow-up.

Subjective: Patient is a 23 nonbinary software engineer at Vargas, Shepard and Porter
(work email mwright@example.net) following up 6 weeks after starting
sertraline 50 mg daily. Reports good response — "the constant edge is
gone." Sleeping 7-8 hours, working from home, recently engaged to partner
Shelley Greene.

No SI/HI. No medication side effects beyond mild initial nausea (resolved
week 2). Therapy weekly with Dr. Brandon Perez (referral from prior
visit). Family hx anxiety (mother Stephanie, treated for GAD).

Objective (telehealth limited): Appears well, well-groomed, eye contact
appropriate via video. Speech normal rate and rhythm. Mood "good," affect
congruent. PHQ-9 administered: 4. GAD-7: 5.

Assessment:
1. GAD — improving on sertraline 50 mg.
2. Health maintenance — due for annual labs.

Plan:

disclaimer per California law.

— Jason Mccormick, MD

into a tree on Hwy 17 at approximately Oct 17, 2025 on 07/28/2022. GCS 13 on EMS
arrival, brought to Chambers Group as nearest facility. EMS run report 2441611665401.
Family notified — mother Stephen Dennis (348-467-0042) en route from
Rodriguez, King and Orozco; father deceased.

- Right pneumothorax with chest tube placed by Dr. Carol Gillespie at Jun 15, 2019
- Grade II splenic laceration (CT abdomen Apr 04, 2026, read by Dr. Mindy Chambers via teleradiology)
- Closed right tib/fib fracture, splinted

PEDIATRIC CLINIC VISIT — WELL-CHILD

Patient: Monica Johnson       DOB: 07/16/2018        Age: 23
MRN: 850570202742589                 Visit date: 03/25/2019 Jun 26, 2018
Provider: Dr. Debra Lloyd, MD (pediatrics)
Clinic: Allen and Sons, 91244 Greene Walks

Accompanied by: Mother (Trevor Ortiz, cell 597.368.1633x99309,
email brittanychavez@example.com — works as a researcher at Alvarado PLC). Father Jason Marks was unable to attend.

Interval history: 4-year well-child visit. Per mom, Cynthia is doing well
in pre-K at Williams, Brown and Hernandez. Eats varied diet, sleeps 11 hours
overnight in her own bed at home (075 Stacy Spur). No concerns from teacher
Ms. Hartwell. Met all developmental milestones. No recent illnesses other
than a brief URI in Apr 27, 2017.

Older brother Timothy (age 23, MRN 457802536930161) had strep throat last
week — Cynthia has not had symptoms.

Vaccines: Up to date. Today receiving DTaP #48, IPV #4, MMR #2, varicella
#2 per CDC schedule. VIS sheets provided (English).

Vitals: Wt 16.8 kg (50%ile), Ht 103 cm (60%ile), BMI 15.8, HR 102,
BP 92/58 (manual, right arm), T 98.0.
Vision: 20/30 R, 20/25 L (Snellen with cards).
Hearing: Passed bilaterally.

Exam: Well-appearing, cooperative. HEENT, cardiac, lung, abdominal,
neuro, MSK exams all within normal limits. Tympanic membranes clear.

Assessment/Plan:
1. Healthy 34. Anticipatory guidance discussed (booster seat
   until 4'9", screen time, oral health — recommend dentist by age 48).
2. Vaccines given as above. Adverse reaction info reviewed.
3. Next visit: 48-year well-child in 12 months. Schedule with front desk.
4. After-visit summary printed; mom prefers paper copy.

Visit time: 25 minutes face-to-face.
— Jessica Jones, MD

document.text:
PSYCHIATRY INTAKE — INITIAL EVALUATION

Patient: Monica Johnson        DOB: 07/16/2018        Age: 23
MRN: 9462737345147                    Date: Oct 28, 2022
Provider: Dr. Lauren Lin, MD
Setting: Outpatient — Rodriguez and Sons, 124 Howard Walks

Source: Self-referral via PCP Dr. Melissa Hebert; PHQ-9 of 18 at
last PCP visit on 07/05/2023.

CC (patient's words): "I haven't felt like myself since the divorce. I
need help."

HPI: 23 transmasculine patient (he/him), works as line cook at
Vargas, Shepard and Porter (downtown 39930 Jessica Stravenue Apt. 394). Reports 4 months of
worsening low mood, anhedonia, sleep disturbance (early-morning awakening
0330-0400), 12-lb weight loss, and passive SI without plan or intent.
Onset followed separation from spouse (Shawn Washington, divorce finalized
12/19/2022). Patient now lives alone at 859909 Rachel Club.

Past psych hx: Depression episode in college (2024-04-22) treated with
fluoxetine, partial response. No prior hospitalizations, no prior
suicide attempts. No SUD history, drinks 2-3 beers on weekends only.

Family hx: Mother David Massey with depression (lives in 751 Mary Dam,
360.414.8670x042, listed as emergency contact with patient permission).
Maternal uncle completed suicide at age 48.

Social: Estranged from biological father. Close friend Sherry (last name
unknown to patient — "Daniel from work") is primary support. T currently on
testosterone cypionate 100mg q week prescribed by Dr. Jon Orozco at
Watson, Lee and Yates since Sep 17, 2020.

Mental status: Casually dressed, fair grooming. Cooperative. Speech
slowed. Mood "tired." Affect blunted, congruent. TC linear. No SI today.
No psychosis. Insight and judgment intact.

Assessment: Major depressive disorder, recurrent, moderate-to-severe,
without psychotic features. PHQ-9 today: 19.

Plan:
- Start sertraline 25 mg daily x 7 days, then 50 mg daily.
- Weekly therapy with Lena Brewer, LCSW (intake call (957)298-6159x43861).
- Safety plan reviewed and documented; 988 reviewed.
- Follow up with me in 3 weeks (02/13/2026 at Apr 06, 2023). Reachable at
  3974068594 or tpage@example.org.
- Coordination of care note faxed to Dr. Edgar 689.711.1517.

Visit duration: 60 minutes (intake CPT 90792).
— Sandra Jennings, MD

document.text:
PATIENT RESULT LETTER

Jan 19, 2025

Anthony Walker
383 John Field Apt. 091
445 Hebert Stravenue

Re: Lab results from your visit on Jun 05, 2021
MRN: 62673195067

Dear Ms. Shelia,

I am writing to share the results of the bloodwork we drew at your annual
physical last week.

Your overall results look good. Your cholesterol panel is improved
compared to last year — total cholesterol 188 (down from 217), LDL 102,
HDL 58, triglycerides 140. Your hemoglobin A1C is 5.4 (normal). Liver
and kidney function are within normal range.

Two values are slightly outside the normal range and worth following:

  - Vitamin D 25-OH: 22 ng/mL (low; goal >30). I recommend starting
    over-the-counter vitamin D3 2000 IU daily. We can recheck in 3 months.

  - TSH: 4.9 mIU/L (mildly elevated; normal 0.4-4.0). This may be a
    one-time finding. Please come in for a repeat thyroid panel in
    6 weeks. You can call our scheduling line at 616.933.9930x93139 or use
    the patient portal.

If you have questions, you can message me through the portal or call
the office. Your appointment for the repeat thyroid panel can be brief —
just a blood draw, no provider visit needed unless you'd like one.

Best wishes,

Kristina Gonzalez, MD
Richards-Wilson
990 Rachel Club Apt. 907
117 Stephen Street Apt. 148
office 616.933.9930x93139 | fax 001-667-500-4212x817

MEDICAL ONCOLOGY FOLLOW-UP

Patient: Monica Johnson    MRN: 0947918871
DOB: 05/29/2024                        Visit date: 03/25/2019 Jun 26, 2018
Provider: Dr. Debra Lloyd, MD, FACP
Cancer center: Allen and Sons, 91244 Greene Walks

Diagnosis: Stage IIIB non-small cell lung adenocarcinoma, EGFR exon 19
deletion, diagnosed 04/07/2021. Currently on osimertinib 80mg daily,
month 18 of treatment.

Interval history: Patient is here with wife Ricky (cell 597.368.1633x99309)
and son Jason Hill (a physician, internal medicine, who has been
helping coordinate care; ugonzalez@example.net). Last imaging
06/19/2018 — continued partial response, primary lesion 1.4 cm (down
from 1.6 cm prior). No new symptoms. Energy good — golfed 9 holes at
Reid-Mendez twice this month. Dry skin and minor paronychia of the right
thumb, managed conservatively per nail care handout.

Side effect review: Diarrhea — 0. Rash — minimal, controlled with
moisturizer. Cardiac — no chest pain, no edema. Last echo 05/02/2022,
LVEF 60%.

Vitals: Wt 78.4 kg (stable). BP 132/78. HR 70. SpO2 97% Jones Inc today (drawn Jul 28, 2022, accession LB-26-44819):
  CBC: WBC 5.8, Hgb 13.4, Plt 224 — normal.
  CMP: Cr 0.9, AST 24, ALT 28 — normal.

Assessment/Plan:
1) NSCLC on osimertinib, sustained partial response — continue current
   dose. No dose modification needed.
2) Repeat CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast in 8 weeks; will order
   now for 12/15/2023 at our imaging center (+1-975-211-7276x01486).
3) Cardiac surveillance — schedule echo in 6 months.
4) Ongoing supportive care — refilled emollient prescription to Adams Inc at
   174 Patricia Rapid, 47657 Wilson Rapid Suite 386 (Rx #88-1024-A).
5) Patient continues to do well. He plans a trip to 8050 Pope Manor to visit his
   brother (Amanda, age 23) in late Aug 31, 2023; cleared for travel,
   reviewed medication supply (will bring extra month).

Patient cell: 394-312-6940x27389. Email: cohennathan@example.com. Pt portal
preferred for non-urgent messages.

Next visit: 12/15/2023 with imaging same day.
— Kevin Brooks, MD
